waves.method: Multi-sensor fusion of S2 L2A NBR + Landsat C2 NBR + S1 RTC dVV + MODIS-09A1 SR NBR + MODIS-14A1 active-fire, has_scar_fused via OR. Temporal persistence from snapshot.db.

burn_scar_fused.evidence: S2 dNBR_max=0.3965 (moderate); Landsat dNBR_max=0.19 (low); S1 dVV=-0.076dB; MODIS-SR=no_data; MODIS-AF=no_data

suggestions.basis: fused burn-scar across 1 sensors (S2 dNBR_max=0.3965 (moderate); Landsat dNBR_max=0.19 (low); S1 dVV=-0.076dB; MODIS-SR=no_data; MODIS-AF=no_data) + signal cooled (no 48h hits) + vision prior 'agricultural_field' -> transient ag burn

waves.method: Resolve remaining unsure cells by combining full-roster fused burn-scar evidence with Claude Sonnet 4.5 vision pass on a 1024px Esri tile + Sentinel-2 false-color (B12/B11/B8A) composite. Vision confidence >= 0.7 wins; else multi-sensor + temporal fallback.

resolutions.fused_evidence: S1 dVV=-0.166dB; S3-FRP-scenes=10; VNP14-granules=22; TROPOMI-CO 8->7; BM-pers=0.3

vision_classification.basis: The optical image shows a dark burn scar pattern with scattered trees and disturbed vegetation typical of wildfire. Multi-sensor evidence supports active burning: moderate Sentinel-1 VV decrease (-0.166dB), 10 S3-FRP detections, 22 VNP14 granules, TROPOMI CO decline, and 7-day persistence all consistent with a recent wildfire event rather than a persistent thermal source.

resolutions.basis: Sonnet vision (conf 0.85): The optical image shows a dark burn scar pattern with scattered trees and disturbed vegetation typical of wildfire. Multi-sensor evidence supports active burning: moderate Sentinel-1 VV decrease (-0.166dB), 10 S3-FRP detections, 22 VNP14 granules, TROPOMI CO decline, and 7-day persistence all consistent with a recent wildfire event rather than a persistent thermal source.

resolutions.fused_evidence: S1 dVV=-0.033dB; S3-FRP-scenes=10; VNP14-granules=23; TROPOMI-CO 8->7; BM-pers=0.3

vision_classification.basis: The false-color composite shows bright cyan rectangular structures characteristic of plastic greenhouse coverings. The top-right corner shows a distinct white/bright structure in the Esri imagery, and the persistent thermal detections (10 S3-FRP scenes, 23 VNP14 granules, BM-pers=0.3) with minimal SAR change (dVV=-0.033dB) are consistent with solar heating of plastic agricultural infrastructure rather than vegetation combustion.

resolutions.basis: Sonnet vision (conf 0.92): The false-color composite shows bright cyan rectangular structures characteristic of plastic greenhouse coverings. The top-right corner shows a distinct white/bright structure in the Esri imagery, and the persistent thermal detections (10 S3-FRP scenes, 23 VNP14 granules, BM-pers=0.3) with minimal SAR change (dVV=-0.033dB) are consistent with solar heating of plastic agricultural infrastructure rather than vegetation combustion.

resolutions.fused_evidence: HLS dNBR=0.2322; S1 dVV=0.126dB; S3-FRP-scenes=10; VNP14-granules=22; TROPOMI-CO 8->8; BM-pers=0.3

vision_classification.basis: The RGB image shows a complex of buildings surrounded by fields with darker disturbed areas visible. The Sentinel-2 false-color composite displays a bright yellow-green signature characteristic of active thermal anomalies or recent burn scars mixed with agricultural land. The multi-sensor evidence strongly supports a fire event: moderate dNBR (0.23), 10 S3-FRP scenes, 22 VNP14 granules, elevated TROPOMI-CO, and persistence over 7 days but not 24-48h, indicating a recent wildfire or large agricultural burn that has subsided. The location and thermal persistence pattern are consistent with a real thermal event rather than a persistent infrared source.

resolutions.basis: Sonnet vision (conf 0.82): The RGB image shows a complex of buildings surrounded by fields with darker disturbed areas visible. The Sentinel-2 false-color composite displays a bright yellow-green signature characteristic of active thermal anomalies or recent burn scars mixed with agricultural land. The multi-sensor evidence strongly supports a fire event: moderate dNBR (0.23), 10 S3-FRP scenes, 22 VNP14 granules, elevated TROPOMI-CO, and persistence over 7 days but not 24-48h, indicating a recent wildfire or large agricultural burn that has subsided. The location and thermal persistence pattern are consistent with a real thermal event rather than a persistent infrared source.

resolutions.fused_evidence: S2 dNBR=0.2388; LS dNBR=0.1575; HLS dNBR=0.1765; S1 dVV=-0.023dB; MODIS-LST Δ=5.45K; S3-FRP-scenes=10; VNP14-granules=23; TROPOMI-CO 8->8; BM-pers=0.7

vision_classification.basis: The top orthophoto shows disturbed/darkened agricultural fields consistent with recent burning. Multi-sensor evidence strongly supports fire: positive dNBR (0.24 S2, 0.16 LS), 10 S3-FRP detections, 23 VNP14 granules, and elevated LST. The dark patches in yellow farmland (false-color) match burn scar signatures rather than persistent infrastructure.

resolutions.basis: Sonnet vision (conf 0.82): The top orthophoto shows disturbed/darkened agricultural fields consistent with recent burning. Multi-sensor evidence strongly supports fire: positive dNBR (0.24 S2, 0.16 LS), 10 S3-FRP detections, 23 VNP14 granules, and elevated LST. The dark patches in yellow farmland (false-color) match burn scar signatures rather than persistent infrastructure.

resolutions.fused_evidence: S2 dNBR=0.1598; LS dNBR=0.2439; HLS dNBR=0.1824; S1 dVV=-0.112dB; S3-FRP-scenes=10; VNP14-granules=21; TROPOMI-CO 8->8; BM-pers=0.7

vision_classification.basis: The Sentinel-2 false-color image shows a distinct dark grey/black patch in the upper-right quadrant, characteristic of solar PV panels with low reflectance in SWIR. The persistent thermal detections (7d active, 10 S3-FRP scenes, 21 VNP14 granules) combined with very low burn-scar indices (dNBR ~0.16-0.24) and minimal radar change confirm a non-fire persistent thermal source typical of solar farms.

resolutions.basis: Sonnet vision (conf 0.92): The Sentinel-2 false-color image shows a distinct dark grey/black patch in the upper-right quadrant, characteristic of solar PV panels with low reflectance in SWIR. The persistent thermal detections (7d active, 10 S3-FRP scenes, 21 VNP14 granules) combined with very low burn-scar indices (dNBR ~0.16-0.24) and minimal radar change confirm a non-fire persistent thermal source typical of solar farms.
